content:
# Geomechanics

**Summary**: The study of the mechanical properties and behavior of rocks and soils.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] evaluated geomechanical characteristics to understand reservoir brittleness and stress state.

- [[2015-own|Own, 2015]] addressed geomechanics by analyzing how rock properties influence fracture propagation.

- [[2015-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2015]] evaluated geomechanical properties including Schmidt Hammer Rebound Number, Point Load Index, and ultrasonic wave velocity.

- [[2016-babker|Babker, 2016]] investigated geomechanical properties in relation to lithostratigraphy.

- [[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016]] evaluated geomechanics to determine brittleness and fracability for hydraulic fracturing design.

- [[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016]] evaluated geomechanics to build 1D MEMs for predicting in-suit stress.

- [[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017]] evaluated geomechanics to understand how lithology influences rock strength.

- [[2017-benaafi|Benaafi, 2017]] evaluated geomechanical properties to understand rock stability and fracture behavior.

- [[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020]] applied geomechanics to determine rock elastic properties (REP) for hydraulic fracturing design.

- [[2022-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2022]] evaluated geomechanics to determine brittleness for fracturing design.

- [[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]] investigated rock mechanics in the Khafji reservoir.

## Scientific Context

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] Geomechanical data is critical for determining the suitability of a shale for hydraulic fracturing.

- [[2015-own|Own, 2015]] addressed geomechanics by analyzing how rock properties influence fracture propagation.

- [[2015-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2015]] evaluated geomechanical properties including Schmidt Hammer Rebound Number, Point Load Index, and ultrasonic wave velocity.

- [[2016-babker|Babker, 2016]] investigated geomechanical properties in relation to lithostratigraphy.

- [[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016]] evaluated geomechanics to determine brittleness and fracability for hydraulic fracturing design.

- [[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016]] evaluated geomechanics to build 1D MEMs for predicting in-suit stress.

- [[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017]] evaluated geomechanics to understand how lithology influences rock strength.

- [[2017-benaafi|Benaafi, 2017]] evaluated geomechanical properties to understand rock stability and fracture behavior.

- [[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020]] applied geomechanics to determine rock elastic properties (REP) for hydraulic fracturing design.

- [[2022-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2022]] evaluated geomechanics to determine brittleness for fracturing design.

- [[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002]] investigated rock mechanics in the Khafji reservoir.

## Measurement and Characterization Methods

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] derived from acoustic wave velocity measurements and uniaxial compression tests.

## Key Findings Across Sources

- Determined Young's modulus, Poisson's ratio, and UCS for the Qusaiba Shale. ([[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015, p. 63]])

- Rock mechanics are fundamental to understanding hydraulic fracturing success. ([[2015-own|Own, 2015, p. 72]])

- Three distinct geomechanical units were established based on variations in strength index, porosity, and wave velocity. ([[2015-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2015, p. 14]])

- Identified five geomechanical units with varying strength levels linked to specific lithofacies. ([[2016-babker|Babker, 2016, p. 13]])

- Average values of Young's modulus and Poisson's ratio indicate high stiffness and brittle nature of the Sarah Formation. ([[2016-ejaz|Ejaz, 2016, p. 14]])

- Lithology is the main control on brittleness in the Khuff Formation. ([[2016-saleh|Saleh, 2016, p. 19]])

- Lithological units were classified based on point load index and uniaxial compressive strength. ([[2017-mahgoub|Mahgoub, 2017, p. 126]])

- Correlation was established between grain size and geomechanical properties like UCS. ([[2017-benaafi|Benaafi, 2017, p. 14]])

- Rock elastic properties are crucial for pore pressure prediction, wellbore stability, and hydraulic fracturing. ([[2020-gbiyanju|Gbiyanju, 2020, p. 47]])

- TML exhibits less brittleness than Hanifa due to higher clay content and more abundant laminations. ([[2022-abdlmutalib|Abdlmutalib, 2022, p. 19]])

- The relationship among the magnitudes of the three principal stresses (sigma1 > sigma2 > sigma3) suggests a normal fault regime. ([[2002-elhassan|ElHassan, 2002, p. 19]])

## Contrasting Values and Interpretations

- [[2015-mustafa|Mustafa, 2015]] Young's modulus, Poisson's ratio, Cohesion, Friction.
